Name: LatencyOptimizedAdaptiveMixedPrecisionViTs
Title: Latency-Optimized Adaptive Mixed-Precision Training for Vision Transformers
Short Hypothesis: Adaptive mixed-precision (AMP) training can significantly reduce latency and memory usage for vision transformers (ViTs) while maintaining or improving model accuracy, especially under latency-critical conditions.
Related Work: Recent works such as AMP-ViT and SNIP have explored adaptive mixed-precision techniques for vision transformers and large language models, respectively. However, these methods often focus on either model efficiency or accuracy, and do not explicitly optimize for latency-critical inference. Our proposal aims to bridge this gap by integrating latency-aware optimization into the adaptive mixed-precision framework, ensuring that the model meets strict latency requirements without compromising accuracy.
Abstract: Vision transformers (ViTs) have achieved state-of-the-art performance in computer vision tasks but suffer from high computational and memory demands. Adaptive mixed-precision (AMP) training has shown promise in improving efficiency, but existing methods often prioritize either model accuracy or efficiency, lacking explicit latency optimization. This proposal introduces Latency-Optimized Adaptive Mixed-Precision Training (LO-AMP) for ViTs, which integrates latency-aware optimization to ensure that the model meets strict latency requirements while maintaining or improving accuracy. LO-AMP dynamically adjusts precision levels based on real-time latency feedback and model performance, using a novel Jacobian-based layer-skipping framework to minimize hidden-state perturbation and enforce strict wall-clock budgets. We validate LO-AMP on ViT, DeiT, and Swin models, demonstrating significant latency reductions and accuracy improvements on the ImageNet dataset.
Experiments: 1. **Latency-Aware Precision Selection**: Develop a latency-aware precision selection algorithm that dynamically adjusts the precision levels of different layers based on real-time latency feedback and model performance. Use a Jacobian-based layer-skipping framework to minimize hidden-state perturbation and enforce strict wall-clock budgets.

2. **Latency-Optimized Training Pipeline**: Integrate the precision selection algorithm into a training pipeline that includes per-channel quantization, BatchNorm folding, and Jacobian-based layer-skipping. Evaluate the pipeline on ViT, DeiT, and Swin models using the ImageNet dataset.

3. **Latency and Accuracy Trade-off Analysis**: Conduct a comprehensive analysis of the latency-accuracy trade-off for different precision configurations. Use metrics such as wall-clock time, memory usage, and accuracy to evaluate model performance.

4. **Latency-Critical Inference Evaluation**: Test the models under latency-critical conditions, such as real-time video processing, to ensure that the models meet strict latency requirements. Compare the performance of LO-AMP with existing methods, including AMP-ViT and SNIP, in terms of latency, memory usage, and accuracy.
Risk Factors and Limitations: 1. **Complexity of Real-Time Feedback**: Implementing real-time latency feedback and dynamic precision adjustment can be complex and may introduce additional overhead. We will mitigate this by using efficient Jacobian-based layer-skipping and strict wall-clock budgets.

2. **Model-Specific Optimizations**: The effectiveness of LO-AMP may vary across different ViT architectures and datasets. We will perform extensive ablation studies to ensure robustness across a range of models and tasks.

3. **Latency-Accuracy Trade-off**: Balancing latency and accuracy is a challenging problem. We will use a Pareto frontier analysis to identify the optimal configurations that provide the best trade-off.
